title: User Management
content:
## Feature: Admin — User Management

### Page URL
Admin page: /admin (default tab: User Management)

### Acceptance Criteria

AC1. The /admin page renders three tabs in order: "User Management" (default 
     active), "Roles & Access", "Audit Log".
AC2. Clicking "Roles & Access" tab shows the Roles & Access view.
     Clicking "User Management" tab returns to the user table.
AC3. The User Management tab header shows three elements simultaneously:
     - "N Users" count label (N = total number of users in the organisation)
     - "Search user" text input (placeholder: "Search user")
     - "Invite user" button (teal background, white text)
AC4. The user table renders exactly five columns in this order:
     User information | Role | Status | Last active | Account Access
AC5. The User information cell renders:
     - Circular avatar: profile photo from SSO if available; otherwise 
       initials monogram (first letter of first name + first letter of 
       last name) with a background colour derived from the user's name
     - If only an email is available (no name): show email address in the 
       avatar area per the mock design (initials fallback using email prefix)
     - Full name in bold (first line), email address in muted text (second line)
     - If no name available: email address on the first line only
AC6. The Role cell shows plain text role label: "Admin", "Finance", 
     "Business", or other role as assigned. No badge styling.
AC7. The Status cell shows:
     - Green "Active" badge for active users
     - Red "Inactive" badge for inactive users
AC8. The Last active cell shows:
     - A relative time string (e.g. "19 days ago", "2 hours ago")
     - "Never" if the user has never logged in
AC9. The Account Access cell contains a toggle switch:
     - Teal/ON: user has access enabled
     - Grey/OFF: user's access is revoked or user is inactive
     - Inactive users always show the toggle as OFF
     - Clicking an ON toggle opens a confirmation dialog:
       Title: "Revoke access?"
       Message: "This will prevent [User Name] from accessing the portal."
       Buttons: "Cancel" (secondary), "Revoke" (red/destructive)
     - Confirming sets the toggle to OFF immediately
     - Cancelling leaves the toggle unchanged
     - Clicking an OFF toggle (for a previously active user) re-enables 
       access immediately — no confirmation required
AC10. The Search user input filters the table in real time:
      - Filtering begins after 1 character is typed
      - Matching is case-insensitive
      - Matches on name OR email (partial match)
      - The "N Users" count label does NOT change during filtering
      - Pagination resets to page 1 when search query changes
AC11. If the search query matches no users, the table body shows:
      "No users found for '[query]'."
      The table header row remains visible.
AC12. The currently logged-in Portal Administrator's row is visible in the 
      table, but their Account Access toggle is permanently disabled:
      - The toggle is non-interactive (cannot be clicked)
      - Hovering the toggle shows tooltip: "You cannot revoke your own access."
AC13. The table is scrollable when user count exceeds the viewport height.
      Pagination: 20 rows per page. Page controls (Previous, page numbers, 
      Next) render below the table only when total users > 20.
      The "Invite user" flow is out of scope for this story.

### User Flows

Login Flow:
User navigates to url : https://qa-atlas.qtsolvdev.com/admin
clicks on salesforce login

**Flow 1 — View User Management tab (default):**
1. Admin logs in and navigates to /admin
2. "User Management" tab is active by default
3. Table renders with correct columns and all users visible
4. "N Users" label shows correct total count

**Flow 2 — Search by name:**
1. Admin is on /admin, User Management tab
2. Admin types "testuserinvite1" in the "Search user" input
3. Table updates in real time showing only users whose name or email 
   contains "testuserinvite1" (case-insensitive)
4. Users not matching are hidden

**Flow 3 — Search with no results:**
1. Admin types "zzznomatch" in the "Search user" input
2. Table body shows: "No users match your filters"
3. Table header row is still visible

**Flow 4 — Revoke account access:**
1. Admin is on /admin, User Management tab
2. Admin locates an active user (teal toggle = ON)
3. Admin clicks the toggle
4. Confirmation dialog appears with user's name and "Revoke" button
5. Admin clicks "Revoke"
6. Dialog closes, toggle changes to grey/OFF
7. User's Status badge changes to "Inactive" (if applicable)

**Flow 5 — Cancel access revocation:**
1. Admin clicks an ON toggle for an active user
2. Confirmation dialog appears
3. Admin clicks "Cancel"
4. Dialog closes, toggle remains ON, no change to user state

**Flow 6 — Re-enable access:**
1. Admin locates a user with grey/OFF toggle
2. Admin clicks the toggle
3. Access is re-enabled immediately (no confirmation)
4. Toggle changes to teal/ON

**Flow 7 — Admin's own row is protected:**
1. Admin is on /admin, User Management tab
2. Admin's own row is visible in the table
3. Admin's toggle is visible but non-interactive (greyed/disabled state)
4. Admin hovers the toggle
5. Tooltip "You cannot revoke your own access." is displayed

**Flow 8 — Switch tabs:**
1. Admin is on /admin showing User Management tab
2. Admin clicks "Roles & Access" tab
3. Roles & Access view renders
4. Admin clicks "User Management" tab
5. User Management table is visible again

**Flow 9 — Pagination (if >20 users):**
1. Admin is on /admin with more than 20 users in the organisation
2. Table shows 20 users on page 1
3. Page controls (Previous, 1, 2, …, Next) are visible below the table
4. Admin clicks "Next"
5. Table shows next set of users; Previous button becomes active

### Edge Cases
- User with no name (email-only account): initials fallback using email prefix
- User who has never logged in: Last active shows "Never"
- Search while on page 2: resets to page 1 when new character is typed
- Exactly 20 users: no pagination controls shown
- Exactly 21 users: pagination controls appear
